exhibits.title: Franchise Agreement (with Lease Rider and Internet, Social Media, and Telephone Assignment)
exhibits.why_it_matters: Master contract governing the franchise relationship. Referenced by Item 17 table sections (4.1 term, 4.2 renewal, 7.3 in-term noncompete, 8.1 training, 9 manuals, 10.2 system changes, 11 marketing/Brand Fund, 12 records/inspection, 13 operating standards, 13.19 technology, 16.1 franchisee termination, 16.2 franchisor termination, 17.1 post-term obligations, 17.3 post-term noncompete, 18.2 transfer, 19 ROFR, 21 indemnification, 22.6 integration/modification, 23 dispute resolution).
exhibits.key_burden_or_function: 15-year term; unlimited franchisor assignment; liquidated damages = 12-month average royalty × months remaining; 2-year/10-mile post-term noncompete; mandatory Charlotte NC mediation-then-arbitration; NC choice of law; $25K transfer damages; broad indemnification; franchisor right to access all franchisee technology data without notice.

exhibits.title: Multi-State Addenda to Franchise Agreement, Multi-Unit Development Agreement, and Disclosure Document
exhibits.why_it_matters: Only three state addenda are present: Illinois, Minnesota, and Virginia. Illinois addendum mandates franchisor defer collection of initial franchise and development fees until pre-opening obligations are met and franchisee begins operating, with the explicit reason 'The Illinois Attorney General's Office imposed this deferral requirement due to our financial condition.' Virginia addendum discloses that the estimated initial investment of $540,700–$869,860 exceeds the franchisor's stockholder's equity of $345,862 as of December 31, 2024, and adds Virginia-specific statutory protections to Item 17. Minnesota addendum provides the standard Minn. Stat. 80C termination notice protections (90/60 days termination, 180 days non-renewal), trademark protection/indemnification language, prohibition on release-as-condition-to-renewal, and anti-waiver language.
exhibits.key_burden_or_function: Limited state coverage; IL financial-condition fee deferral; VA stockholder's equity disclosure; MN termination/non-renewal notice protections.

exhibits.title: Multi-Unit Development Agreement (with Ohio Addendum)
exhibits.why_it_matters: Contractual framework for two- or three-unit developer. Defines development schedule obligation, background statement, Section 5 default/termination by franchisor. Ohio addendum provides Ohio-specific provisions for Ohio residents and franchisees with development rights in Ohio.
exhibits.key_burden_or_function: Franchise fee front-loading at MUDA signing ($35K second, $25K third); development-schedule default as grounds for MUDA termination; sign second FA within 12 months of first-school opening, third FA within 24 months of second-school opening; no additional territorial protection granted under MUDA.

exhibits.title: List of State Administrators
exhibits.why_it_matters: State franchise regulator contact list; enumerates state regulatory authorities including Virginia.

exhibits.title: List of State Agents for Service of Process
exhibits.why_it_matters: Identifies state agents for service of process (e.g., Rhode Island Division of Securities, 1511 Pontiac Ave, Cranston RI).

exhibits.title: Unlimited Guaranty and Assumption of Obligations
exhibits.why_it_matters: Personal guaranty form to be signed by owners of the franchisee entity. Joint and several unlimited guaranty of all obligations under the Franchise Agreement. Each Guarantor consents and agrees broadly.
exhibits.key_burden_or_function: Owners personally guarantee all monetary and non-monetary obligations; unlimited; no carve-outs visible in extracted text.

exhibits.title: Form of General Release
exhibits.why_it_matters: Template release required at renewal and at transfer. Explicitly excludes California Civil Code §1542-type waivers unless state law permits.
exhibits.key_burden_or_function: Required signature as condition of renewal and transfer approval.

exhibits.title: Form of Nondisclosure, Non-Solicitation, and Noncompete Agreement (two separate forms)
exhibits.why_it_matters: Two distinct agreements: (1) Nondisclosure and Non-Solicitation Agreement (p155–160) for certain personnel; (2) Nondisclosure and Non-Competition Agreement (p161–166) for CEO and School Administrator. Must be signed by owners, officers, School Administrator, CEO, and certain designated personnel.
exhibits.key_burden_or_function: Two-tier restrictive covenant structure; competitive-business definition; required signatures as condition of operating franchise.

exhibits.title: Table of Contents of Confidential Brand Standards Manual
exhibits.why_it_matters: Required reference showing scope of operations manual. One-page exhibit.
exhibits.key_burden_or_function: Binding specifications; modifications from time to time are binding on franchisee; franchisor may modify at any time in sole discretion.

exhibits.title: Financial Statements (FY2022, FY2023, FY2024)
exhibits.why_it_matters: Audited financial statements for three fiscal years are physically present but the text layer is image-only in this extraction path; no line items, auditor identity, audit opinion, or going-concern language can be confirmed through text extraction. Item 21 body text confirms that audited statements for periods ending December 31, 2022, December 31, 2023, and December 31, 2024 are attached and that fiscal year ends in December. Virginia addendum (Exhibit B) discloses stockholder's equity of $345,862 as of December 31, 2024. Item 8 body discloses franchisor 2024 total revenue of $2,171,363. The Special Risks cover explicitly states 'The Franchisor's financial condition as reflected in its financial statements (see Item 21) calls into question the Franchisor's financial ability to provide services and support to you.' The Illinois addendum imposes fee deferral 'due to our financial condition.' These structural signals suggest meaningful going-concern or weak-balance-sheet language likely exists in the Exhibit J statements, but the actual statement text is not directly surfaced.
exhibits.key_burden_or_function: Financial condition is a disclosed Special Risk; triggered IL fee deferral; VA disclosure; pending PE acquisition (Aug 29, 2025) post-dates the FY2024 financial statement period.

exhibits.exhibit_label: Exhibit K (TOC label) / physical header reads 'EXHIBIT L'
exhibits.title: Current and Former Franchisees (list with Schools Not Yet Opened and Former Franchisees)
exhibits.why_it_matters: Full list of open-school franchisees with names, school territory, physical addresses, and phone numbers (as of January 1, 2025). Multi-unit developers flagged with asterisk. Separate section for 'Schools Not Yet Opened' showing ~39 pending developers with city/state, mostly multi-unit. Separate section for 'Former Franchisees' listing eight mutual or pre-opening terminations plus three franchisees who ceased communications. Contradiction preserved: TOC (p7) lists this as Exhibit K; Item 20 body (p53) refers to 'Exhibit K'; however the physical header on pages 198–201 reads 'EXHIBIT L TO THE DISCLOSURE DOCUMENT' and the page 2 'How to Use' Q&A refers to 'Exhibit L' for the franchisee list.
exhibits.key_burden_or_function: Diligence data source: direct franchisee contact, unopened backlog detail, attrition signal. Signals: 42 signed-not-opened backlog (Item 20 Table 5); 8 named former franchisees in 2024 and 2025 mostly mutual termination pre-opening; 3 ceased-communications franchisees.

exhibits.exhibit_label: Exhibit L (TOC label) / physical header also reads 'EXHIBIT L'
exhibits.title: Franchise Disclosure Questionnaire
exhibits.why_it_matters: Acknowledgment questionnaire signed by prospect at point of sale. Includes affirmation of receipt of FDD 14+ days before signing, no unauthorized statements, no earnings claims outside Item 19. Carries header '[QUESTIONNAIRE NOT FOR USE WITH FRANCHISEES SUBJECT TO THE FRANCHISE LAWS OF ILLINOIS, INDIANA, MINNESOTA, VIRGINIA, OR WISCONSIN]' — acknowledging that certain states prohibit acknowledgment/waiver questionnaires.
exhibits.key_burden_or_function: Acknowledgment of receipt and no-unauthorized-representations; excluded from use in IL, IN, MN, VA, WI.

exhibits.title: Receipt (two copies — one for franchisor, one for franchisee)
exhibits.why_it_matters: FTC Franchise Rule receipt with state-specific 14-day/10-business-day notices (NY, IA, MI). Lists franchise sellers Drew and Jennifer McWilliams. Declares NC registered agent at 9801 Suzanne Court, Weddington, NC 28173.

exhibits.exhibit_label: State Effective Dates (unlettered page, placed between Exhibit L questionnaire and Exhibit M)
exhibits.title: State Effective Dates Summary
exhibits.why_it_matters: Lists registration status across 14 states. Shows CA, HI, MD, NY, ND, RI, SD, WA 'Not registered'; IN effective 3/28/2025; MN effective 5/9/2025; IL, MI, VA, WI 'Pending'. Wisconsin is listed as Pending on this page even though the source PDF was filed with WI-DFI.
